name: scroll-position-preservation
description: Terminal.process() does NOT reset scrollback to 0. Scroll position is preserved when new PTY output arrives. Use reset_scroll() to jump to live view.

name: alt-screen-detection
description: Terminal.is_alt_screen() returns true when a full-screen app is running via alternate screen. Mouse scroll events should be forwarded to PTY (via encode_mouse_scroll()) when in alt screen, not handled for TUI history scrolling.

name: workspace-overlay-rendering
description: When AppMode::WorkspaceOverlay is active, render_daemon_app renders the overlay into main_area (replacing sidebar+terminal) rather than as a popup overlay. The hint bar is always rendered regardless of mode.

name: workspace-overlay-scroll
description: WorkspaceOverlayState.visible_height is stored in the struct (default 20) and updated from the event loop in run_attached() before each key event using last_size and last_hint_bar_height. This allows select_next() to scroll when selection exceeds scroll_offset + visible_height without needing a mutable render function.

name: workspace-overlay-inline-editing
description: render_workspace_overlay uses a virtual index system where index 0 is the draft row (when drafting_workspace.is_some()) and indices 1..N map to workspaces[0..N-1]. scroll_offset and selected_index are in virtual coordinate space. When pressing 'n', reset selected_index=0 and scroll_offset=0 so the draft row is immediately visible and selected at the top. Hint bar updates to show 'enter Create/esc Cancel' or 'enter Rename/esc Cancel' based on overlay substate.

name: session-name-live-wrapping
description: Session name wrapping during edit/rename: build_sidebar_lines uses wrap_session_name with the current rename/draft text (not original session name), so row counts and text slices reflect what is being typed. render_session_list always renders the pre-wrapped 'text' slice. cursor_line_col() helper converts character-offset cursor_position to (line_index, col_in_line) accounting for line wrapping.

name: zoom-mode
description: AppState.zoomed=true hides sidebar and gives terminal full main_area. EventResult::ToggleZoom toggles state.zoomed before returning. compute_term_cols(width, zoomed) adjusts sidebar_width to 0 when zoomed. Triggered by Ctrl+Z in terminal mode only; auto-cleared by focus_sidebar() and enter_create_mode().

name: zoom-overlay-interaction
description: Zoom mode is preserved through workspace overlay open/close — this is correct behavior per spec. The spec only defines two ways to unzoom: Ctrl+Z (toggle) and Ctrl+B (focus sidebar). Opening the workspace overlay does NOT clear zoom; WorkspaceOverlay render takes priority over zoom in render_daemon_app, so the overlay displays correctly regardless of zoom state. Do not add zoom-clearing to workspace overlay open/close.

name: create-mode-drafting
description: handle_create_mode_key() calls start_drafting(session_type) to enter AppMode::Drafting — user types name and presses Enter to create or Esc to cancel. No auto-generated names. handle_drafting_key() handles text input. Sidebar renders draft row at top while drafting.
Rename session spec bug: input_handler.rs was restoring previous_focus (sidebar) on Enter confirm instead of focusing Terminal. Spec says 'Exit rename mode and focus on the terminal pane.'
resolution: Set self.focus = Focus::Terminal on Enter in handle_renaming_key. Esc cancel correctly restores previous_focus. Updated unit test assertion from Focus::Sidebar to Focus::Terminal.

name: workspace-switch-focus
description: After switching to an empty workspace via overlay (Enter), terminal pane is focused (not sidebar). In welcome state, use Ctrl+N (ASCII 14) not 'n' to enter create mode. 'n' only works when sidebar is explicitly focused.

name: npm-binary-distribution
description: npm binary distribution pattern for Rust CLIs: use optionalDependencies with platform packages (os/cpu fields), Node.js launcher script uses require.resolve() to find binary. Key references cloned to references/npm-binary-distribution/ (biome is best Rust example). See references/npm-binary-distribution/README.md for code templates.

name: github-actions-ci-rust
description: GitHub Actions CI/CD for Rust multi-platform builds. Key: (1) macos-latest = macOS 15 arm64, use macos-15-large for Intel. (2) Use cross via taiki-e/install-action@cross for Linux musl; not needed for macOS/Windows. (3) Separate ci.yml (cargo test --lib on PRs) and release.yml (tag-triggered). (4) Release pattern: create draft -> matrix build -> upload to release -> npm publish -> undraft. References at references/github-actions-ci/

name: self-update-research
description: self-update research: use 'update-informer' crate for daily GitHub API version checks with XDG file-mtime cache (etcetera crate for XDG dirs), and 'self_update' crate for binary download+replacement. Homebrew detection: check current_exe() path for /homebrew/, /Homebrew/, /linuxbrew/ or HOMEBREW_PREFIX env var. References in references/self-update/. Detailed notes at references/self-update/RESEARCH_NOTES.md.

name: self-update-impl
description: Self-update mechanism: src/updater.rs has check_and_notify() (called on startup in background thread) and run_self_update() (sb self-update subcommand). Uses ureq for HTTP, self_update crate for binary replacement, manual JSON cache at XDG_CACHE_HOME/sidebar-tui/update-check.json (24h TTL). Homebrew detection checks exe path for /homebrew/ or HOMEBREW_PREFIX env var — prints brew upgrade message instead of self-updating.
release.yml used macos-15-large for x86_64-apple-darwin build but that runner doesn't exist; correct runner for Intel macOS builds is macos-13 (the last Intel-based GitHub Actions runner)
resolution: Change os to macos-13 for x86_64-apple-darwin matrix entry

content: GitHub Actions runner for x86_64-apple-darwin (Intel macOS) builds is 'macos-13'; 'macos-latest' points to arm64 macOS 15 and is correct for aarch64-apple-darwin
In release.yml, the publish-release job (which undrafts the GitHub Release) must depend on publish-npm to avoid the release going public before npm packages are ready

release.yml publish-npm binary injection loop must handle both .tar.gz (macOS/Linux) and .zip (Windows) archives. Use 'elif [ -f dist/${archive_base}.zip ]' with unzip to extract sb.exe for the win32-x64 npm package.

release.yml publish-npm binary injection loop must handle both .tar.gz (macOS/Linux) and .zip (Windows). Use 'elif [ -f dist/${archive_base}.zip ]' branch with 'unzip -q' (available on ubuntu-latest runners) to extract sb.exe and copy it into the win32-x64 npm package dir.

release.yml publish-npm binary injection loop: .zip branch uses 'unzip -q <archive>.zip -d dist/' then find 'sb.exe' — no chmod+x needed for Windows exe. The .tar.gz branch matches both 'sb' and 'sb.exe' with -o; the .zip branch only matches 'sb.exe'.

Windows TCP IPC: daemon uses TcpListener bound to 127.0.0.1:0 (OS assigns port), writes port to daemon.port lockfile. Clients read port from lockfile to connect via TcpStream. IpcStream type alias = UnixStream on Unix, TcpStream on Windows. get_socket_path() returns daemon.sock on Unix, daemon.port on Windows.

Windows IPC: daemon.rs TcpListener binds 127.0.0.1:0 in run_platform(); stream setup (set_nonblocking + set_read_timeout) happens in run_platform() before spawning handle_client thread — handle_client receives already-configured IpcStream. Port written to daemon.port lockfile via write_daemon_port(). Clients read lockfile in DaemonClient::connect_to() and cmd_attach(). IpcStream is pub type alias in daemon.rs, imported in main.rs.

Homebrew tap binary formula patterns: cargo-dist generates formulas with 'if OS.mac? { if Hardware::CPU.arm? }' nesting + per-arch URL/sha256. The mislav/bump-homebrew-formula-action CANNOT update multi-arch binary tap formulas (it only handles single url+sha256). For binary taps, use cargo-dist or a custom script to regenerate the formula on each release. Key reference: references/homebrew-aur/cargo-dist-tap-formula-example.rb

AUR binary package pattern: use 'sidebar-tui-bin' with source_x86_64/source_aarch64 arrays and sha256sums_x86_64/sha256sums_aarch64. Automate with KSXGitHub/github-actions-deploy-aur (needs AUR_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, AUR_USERNAME, AUR_EMAIL secrets). Key references: references/homebrew-aur/aur-gitui-bin-PKGBUILD (multi-arch), references/homebrew-aur/aur-deploy-workflow-sane-fmt.yml (GH Actions job publish_aur_package at line 622)

Homebrew tap formula for binary distribution: use OS.mac?/OS.linux? with Hardware::CPU.arm?/intel? conditionals for per-arch URLs. Formula lives in homebrew/Formula/sb.rb, tap repo is ftl-tools/homebrew-sidebar-tui. Fully regenerated by publish-homebrew job in release.yml on each release. AUR package is sidebar-tui-bin with source_x86_64/source_aarch64; published via KSXGitHub/github-actions-deploy-aur@v3.0.1. mislav/bump-homebrew-formula-action NOT suitable for per-arch binary formulas.

description: set_read_timeout EINVAL on macOS Unix domain sockets: calling set_read_timeout on an accepted UnixStream returns EINVAL (os error 22) on macOS. This happened when the call was moved from inside the client-handler thread into the accept loop — the ? propagated to kill the whole daemon on first connection.
resolution: Move stream setup (set_nonblocking + set_read_timeout) into the spawned thread closure. Use if-let-Err for set_read_timeout (log and continue) since handle_client can function without the timeout. Only set_nonblocking failure should abort the thread.

description: Mouse scroll went through command history instead of scrolling terminal content: macOS Terminal.app converts scroll gestures to Up/Down arrow keys when mouse capture is disabled, which navigate shell command history instead of terminal scrollback.
resolution: Enable mouse capture (EnableMouseCapture) at TUI startup. Set mouse_mode = true as the default in AppState::default(). This ensures scroll events always come as mouse events to crossterm rather than being converted to arrow keys by the OS terminal.

content: Mouse scroll mode is now ON by default (mouse_mode = true, EnableMouseCapture called at startup). ctrl+s toggles to text select mode (DisableMouseCapture). When mouse capture is enabled: non-alt-screen → scrolls TUI scrollback; alt-screen → forwards SGR mouse sequences to PTY.

name: performance-e2e-test
description: To measure command execution time inside TUI vs direct shell: use a sentinel echo (e.g. 'cmd && echo DONE_12345') and wait_for_text() to detect completion. Measure baseline with std::process::Command, assert overhead <= 10s max. Check tool availability with 'which <tool>' before testing.

name: vitepress-video-hero-pattern
description: VitePress is used by Starship (a Rust CLI/TUI tool) for their marketing/docs site. Key pattern: embed a terminal recording video with raw HTML5 video tag directly in index.md after the frontmatter block. Use muted autoplay loop playsinline attributes. CSS: max-width: 700px; border-radius: 6px; margin: 50px auto. No custom Vue component needed for this approach. See references/vitepress-website/ for full research.

name: terminal-recording-tools
description: VHS (charmbracelet/vhs) is the recommended tool for recording sidebar-tui demos. Generates MP4+WebM from a .tape DSL script — scriptable, reproducible, no JS required at page-load. Embed in VitePress #home-hero-image slot via <video autoplay muted loop playsinline>. File sizes: MP4 ~150-400 KB vs GIF ~4-10 MB for 12s demo. Requires ttyd + ffmpeg + Chromium (use Docker on CI: docker run --rm -v $PWD:/vhs ghcr.io/charmbracelet/vhs demo.tape). asciinema-player is good for interactive docs pages but needs ~450 KB JS bundle + dynamic import inside onMounted to avoid SSR crash in VitePress.

content: VitePress for sidebar-tui marketing site: references saved to references/vitepress-website/. Key pattern: Starship-style hero (frontmatter + raw <video> tag below frontmatter in index.md) is simplest. Code groups use ::: code-group syntax. GitHub Pages deploy via .github/workflows/deploy.yml. CSS dark theming via --vp-c-* variable overrides in custom.css.

Terminal recording for website hero: use VHS (charmbracelet/vhs) generating MP4+WebM, embed as <video muted autoplay loop playsinline> in VitePress. Refs at references/terminal-recording/. File sizes: GIF 4-10 MB vs MP4/WebM 100-400 KB. asciinema-player requires dynamic import in onMounted() to avoid VitePress SSR build failures.

name: dynamic-quit-path
description: get_quit_path_for_state() returns 'ctrl + b → q Quit' when terminal focused, 'esc → q Quit' in Renaming/Drafting/CreateMode, 'q Quit' when sidebar focused or in WorkspaceOverlay. E2E test: test_hint_bar_dynamic_quit_path.

content: All distribution artifacts (src/updater.rs, dist/install.sh, homebrew/Formula/sb.rb, .github/workflows/release.yml) must use ftl-tools as the GitHub owner — this matches the actual git remote (https://github.com/ftl-tools/sidebar_tui.git)

name: pty-resize-in-tests
description: PTY resize in e2e tests: use session.session.get_process_mut().set_window_size(cols, rows) to resize the PTY (ptyprocess crate). Send SIGWINCH via 'std::process::Command::new("kill").args(["-WINCH", &pid.to_string()])' where pid = session.session.get_process_mut().pid().as_raw(). Update vt100 parser: session.parser = vt100::Parser::new(rows, cols, 0).

content: VitePress requires 'type: module' in package.json (ESM-only package). Without it, vitepress build fails with 'resolved to an ESM file. ESM file cannot be loaded by require'.

name: vitepress-hero-video
description: VitePress hero video pattern: use dynamic :src bindings in Vue component (set via ref + onMounted) instead of static src attributes, so Vite does not try to resolve video file paths as imports at build time. Use withBase() from vitepress to compute the runtime URL.
